symptoms.symptom_name: Chronic dry cough for more than 8 weeks, post-viral onset

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Neurogenic chronic cough / laryngeal sensory neuropathy
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Chronicity beyond 8 weeks with post-viral onset is characteristic of a post-viral cough hypersensitivity syndrome.

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Neurogenic chronic cough / laryngeal sensory neuropathy
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Laryngeal paraesthesia is a hallmark of the sensory-neuropathic cough phenotype.

symptoms.symptom_name: Allotussia — cough triggered by talking, laughing or cool air
relieving_factors: hard swallow then a sip of water

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Neurogenic chronic cough / laryngeal sensory neuropathy
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Cough provoked by normally non-tussive stimuli indicates a hypersensitive cough reflex.

symptoms.symptom_name: Witnessed transient loss of consciousness during a coughing paroxysm (cough syncope)
symptoms.onset: tonight, at the height of a violent cough
symptoms.character: brief LOC with spontaneous rapid recovery, groggy afterwards

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Neurogenic chronic cough / laryngeal sensory neuropathy
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Syncope at the peak of a violent cough paroxysm fits cough (situational) syncope via a Valsalva mechanism.
Any syncope in an older adult is a red flag requiring ECG/cardiac exclusion before it can be attributed to a benign situational cause.

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Neurogenic chronic cough / laryngeal sensory neuropathy
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Transient hoarseness reflects laryngeal mechanical irritation from repeated violent coughing.

symptoms.symptom_name: Refractoriness to inhaled corticosteroid, PPI and nasal-spray trials

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Failure of an adequate inhaled-corticosteroid trial argues against asthmatic cough.
Failure of a full PPI trial argues against reflux as the cause.

symptoms.symptom_name: Normal breathing between fits — no wheeze or breathlessness at rest

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of interval wheeze/dyspnoea argues against an acute asthma exacerbation as the driver.

symptoms.symptom_name: Chest clear to auscultation, no wheeze or stridor, normal air entry

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A clear chest with no wheeze/stridor argues strongly against acute lower-airway obstruction — but requires clinician auscultation, unavailable via telehealth.

symptoms.symptom_name: Normal heart sounds and (anticipated) normal 12-lead ECG

investigation_details.expected_result: No arrhythmia or structural signs — required to exclude cardiac syncope
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: A cardiac/arrhythmic cause of the syncope would remain unexcluded — the single most important reason this cannot be cleared remotely.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: An ECG is mandatory to exclude an arrhythmic cause of syncope; the AI Doctor cannot obtain one.

symptoms.symptom_name: Anticipated normal chest X-ray and pulmonary function tests

investigation_details.expected_result: Normal — completes the diagnostic tetrad excluding a pulmonary cause
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: A structural or obstructive pulmonary cause would remain unexcluded.

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Neurogenic chronic cough / laryngeal sensory neuropathy
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Excluding pulmonary pathology is required before attributing the cough to a sensory neuropathy (a diagnosis of exclusion).

symptoms.symptom_name: Posterior pharyngeal erythema (mechanical irritation from coughing)

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Neurogenic chronic cough / laryngeal sensory neuropathy
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Erythema reflects chronic mechanical irritation, not a specific aetiology; requires oropharyngeal exam.

symptoms.symptom_name: Haemodynamically stable vitals (BP/HR/SpO2/RR within normal limits)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Normal clinician-measured vitals are reassuring but do not exclude an intermittent arrhythmia; the AI cannot measure them anyway.

symptoms.symptom_name: No haemoptysis, no central cyanosis, no chest pain (pertinent negatives)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of haemoptysis, pleuritic chest pain and hypoxia lowers the probability of PE as the syncope cause.

symptom_clusters.target_diagnosis: Neurogenic chronic cough / laryngeal sensory neuropathy
symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Chronic post-viral dry cough + laryngeal paraesthesia + allotussia together strongly indicate a sensory-neuropathic cough hypersensitivity, especially when refractory to standard therapy.

investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Mandatory to exclude an arrhythmic cause of the syncope in an older adult; the pivotal in-person step the AI Doctor cannot perform.
Helps distinguish orthostatic from situational/cardiac syncope; requires in-person measurement.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Chest X-ray and pulmonary function tests
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Completes exclusion of a pulmonary cause of chronic cough before attributing it to a sensory neuropathy.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Empirical repeat of inhaled corticosteroid or high-dose PPI
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Already trialled and failed; repeating them would delay the correct neuromodulator/speech-therapy pathway and is not the AI's role to initiate.
